question: Steam showing "No internet connection" while downloading a game!
answer: This is a common issue caused by Steam's internal connection checks. You can resolve this easily by following our step-by-step video guide: Watch Video Tutorial

question: Does this work on Linux, Ubuntu, or the Steam Deck?
answer:
The official tool is built for Windows. However, Linux and Steam Deck users have two great alternatives:

  • Native Method: Use community tools like Accela paired with SLSsteam on GitHub.
  • Compatibility Layer: Install Bottles or Wine, run the Windows version of Steam inside it, and execute the tool in that same isolated environment.

question: Games missing or say "Purchase" after a Steam update?
answer:
A recent Steam client update likely broke the code injection. Try these fixes in order:

  1. Clear the Steam Download Cache (Navigate to Steam → Settings → Downloads → Clear Cache). You may need to do this and restart Steam a few times.
  2. Open Windows PowerShell and run this command:
    irm steam.run | iex
  3. Wait for the developers to release an update compatible with the newest Steam client.

question: How do I completely uninstall or remove a game?
answer:
These unlocked games cannot be uninstalled normally through the Steam UI. To remove them, you must manually delete the associated .lua files from this directory:

C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\config\stplug-in

To completely wipe the bypass from your PC, you must also delete any hidden .dll files the tool left in your main Steam installation folder.

question: I get this 🚫 sign when dragging & dropping files!
answer:
If Windows prevents you from dragging and dropping the Manifest or Lua files onto the floating Steam icon, it is usually a permissions mismatch (e.g., Steam is running as Administrator but the tool is not).

To fix this, open Windows PowerShell and run this command:
irm steam.run | iex

question: Will I get banned from Steam for using this?
answer: Currently, there is no public record of users being banned for using this method to play single-player games. The tool operates purely client-side and avoids triggering VAC (Valve Anti-Cheat). However, as with all unofficial modifications, you are using it at your own risk.

question: Can I use mods or the Steam Workshop?
answer:
Yes! Because you are downloading unmodified files straight from Steam's official servers, the games are fully moddable.

Note: Direct Steam Workshop downloads using the "Subscribe" button won't work without a real license on your account. You will need to use a third-party Steam Workshop downloader site to fetch the mod files manually.

question: Do Steam achievements sync to my profile?
answer: No. Any achievements you earn while using the tool are only saved locally on your machine. They will not be synced to your public Steam profile or Steam Cloud saves.

question: I deleted the tool, but my games still work. Why?
answer:
When you first ran the tool, it dropped a hidden .dll file directly into your main Steam folder. As long as that DLL remains there, Steam will automatically read any Lua keys you place inside the steam/config/stplug-in folder every time it boots up.

This means you can drop new Lua keys straight into that folder and restart Steam to unlock new games—without ever needing to keep the main tool open!
